BombVault can now back up **itself**. This closes the last disaster-recovery gap: after the guided Recovery tab (v4.1) and the credential-complete recovery kit (v4.1.1), a rebuilt Unraid box can now restore BombVault's own settings, targets and off-site credentials in one shot.

## Added

- **A new "Config" backup domain — BombVault backs up its own `/config`.** Its settings database, off-site credentials (`rclone.conf`) and libvirt SSH keypair are captured as a single restic backup: scheduled or on demand, off-site-replicable and append-only-capable like every other domain, with no container stop. The snapshot is taken with SQLite's `VACUUM INTO`, so a WAL-mode database is never captured mid-write. Enable it under Settings → Domains ("App configuration"); it then gets its own Config tab. (#26, thanks @manilx)
- **Restore BombVault's own settings from the Recovery tab.** On a rebuilt server, a new first step restores BombVault's configuration before anything else — so the backup paths, off-site targets and credentials the rest of the recovery flow needs come pre-filled. Because the running process holds its settings database open, the restore is staged and applied on a restart: BombVault restarts its own container over the Docker socket and the page reloads when it's back (or tells you to restart it manually if it can't). The staged database is only swapped in at boot, before it is opened, so the live database is never overwritten under an open handle.

## Changed

- **The recovery kit now lists the config repository location** — the one bootstrap value a fresh install still needs to find its own settings backup (the same role the master key already plays). Config backups also appear in the dashboard history heatmap and the protection scorecard, and an append-only config off-site is verified by the scheduled tamper test like the other domains.

---
